title: Treaty of Brunevale
content:
The Treaty of Brunevale (1965) was a diplomatic agreement in which Willa Briar, foreign minister of Republic of Dorsaly, helped finalize terms at Zephyr Bay.

Background.

By the early 1965s, diplomats from Republic of Dorsaly sought a settlement after years of border incidents near Zephyr Bay.

Reactions.

Newspapers in Republic of Dorsaly praised the compromise, while opposition leaders argued that naval access points near Zephyr Bay were not sufficiently protected.

Signing and terms.

The Treaty of Brunevale was signed in Zephyr Bay on 14 September 1965. The agreement established trade corridors and a joint commission chaired initially by Willa Briar.

Historical assessment.

Modern historians view the Treaty of Brunevale as a stabilizing milestone, crediting Willa Briar's insistence on verifiable inspection clauses.
